sections.heading: Problem Statement
sections.content: Once a section is created, its ID cannot be changed without creating a new section and deleting the old one. This loses history and breaks any edges or links pointing to the old section ID. Agents using `cortex_update_section` can change a section's heading and content but have no way to fix a bad or auto-generated slug.

sections.heading: User Stories
sections.content:
**US-RS-01 — Rename section ID via MCP**
As an agent, I want to pass a `new_id` parameter to `cortex_update_section` so that I can rename a section's slug without losing its history or breaking links.

**US-RS-02 — Cascading reference updates**
As an agent, I want all database references (doc_sections, edges, history) to update when a section ID changes so that nothing breaks silently.

**US-RS-03 — Rename validation**
As an agent, I want the rename to fail with a clear error if the new ID collides with an existing section in the same document so that I don't create duplicates.

sections.heading: Current Capabilities
sections.content:
| Capability | Status |
|---|---|
| `new_id` parameter on `cortex_update_section` | Done |
| Cascading rename in doc_sections table | Done |
| Cascading rename in edges table | Done |
| Cascading rename in history table | Done |
| Update DocJSON file on disk with new ID | Done |
| Duplicate ID validation on rename | Done |

sections.heading: Backlog
sections.content: **Viz slug rename integration** — The auto-slug feature (sibling sub-feature) allows editing slugs in the browser. Currently this only changes the ID in the in-memory doc before save. Once `cortex_update_section` supports `new_id`, the viz could use it for atomic renames with proper cascading — but this is a future enhancement. For v1, the viz save endpoint already writes the full doc, so the rename is handled by the re-index on save.
